# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, max(x.width, PAD))
}
```

## (b) Lemma specification with `ensures` clauses

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); Wellformed(c)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.kind == x.kind
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.value == x.value
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.width >= PAD
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); Canonicalize(c) == c
{ }
```

**Key points:**

- The kind and value are **never** changed (ensures 2 and 3)
- The width becomes `max(x.width, PAD)` — at least 4 digits (ensure 4)
- If already at 4+ digits, the width is unchanged (ensure 5)
- If below 4 digits, the width becomes exactly 4 (ensure 6)
- The result is wellformed (ensure 1)
- Idempotency: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op (ensure 7)
